# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Deploy to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

concurrency:
  group: production-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build_test:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build --if-present

      - name: Test
        run: npm test

  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build_test
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy
        env:
          DEPLOY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}
          DEPLOY_HOST: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -z "${DEPLOY_TOKEN:-}" ] && { [ -z "${DEPLOY_HOST:-}" ] || [ -z "${DEPLOY_USER:-}" ] || [ -z "${DEPLOY_PATH:-}" ]; }; then
            echo "Missing deployment configuration. Set DEPLOY_TOKEN or (DEPLOY_HOST, DEPLOY_USER, DEPLOY_PATH) secrets."
            exit 1
          fi

          if [ -n "${DEPLOY_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
            curl -fsSL -X POST \
              -H "Authorization: Bearer ${DEPLOY_TOKEN}" \
              -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
              -d "{\"ref\":\"${GITHUB_SHA}\"}" \
              "${DEPLOY_HOST}"
          else
            echo "Using SSH deploy via rsync to ${DEPLOY_USER}@${DEPLOY_HOST}:${DEPLOY_PATH}"
            sudo apt-get update -y
            sudo apt-get install -y rsync openssh-client
            mkdir -p ~/.ssh
            ssh-keyscan -H "${DEPLOY_HOST}" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts
            rsync -az --delete ./ "${DEPLOY_USER}@${DEPLOY_HOST}:${DEPLOY_PATH}"
            ssh "${DEPLOY_USER}@${DEPLOY_HOST}" "cd '${DEPLOY_PATH}' && (npm ci || true) && (npm run build --if-present || true) && (pm2 reload all || true)"
          fi
